mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and boosts equity multiples, so month-over-month declines in policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months with declining FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03 after conditioning on rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation accelerates and FEDFUNDS remains near zero, real rates become more negative and policy stays easy enough to support risk assets, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation rises above its prior 3-month average while FEDFUNDS stays below 0.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the signal does not produce positive 20-day SPX returns more often than baseline after the trigger, or if returns are negative when inflation rises and the policy rate remains below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals weakening labor income and earnings pressure, which increases recession risk and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show negative 20-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time, or if the signal loses predictive power after repeated unemployment rises.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in retail sales relative to payroll growth indicates demand outpacing labor income, which supports near-term corporate revenue surprises and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth exceeds PAYEMS growth over the same monthly observation window
failure_condition: Reject if periods with retail sales growth above payroll growth do not lead to positive 5-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term adds no incremental predictive value versus either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals improving real activity and operating leverage for cyclicals, which supports S&P 500 gains over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive monthly changes in industrial production fail to associate with above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the rolling 3-year IC is consistently indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in housing starts signals tightening financial conditions and weaker future construction demand, which should pull S&P 500 returns lower over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average by more than 10%
failure_condition: Reject if deep housing starts declines are not followed by below-average 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect reverses during repeated housing contractions.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP and payroll growth improve together, broad final demand and labor income are both accelerating, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and PAYEMS both rise versus their previous releases
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous GDP and payroll improvement does not outperform single-series signals for 60-day SPX returns, or if the joint condition fails to produce positive returns in a majority of episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a still-elevated unemployment rate combined with rising policy rates signals policy restraint is biting into growth, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises while UNRATE is above 5.5%
failure_condition: Reject if rate hikes with unemployment above 5.5% do not lead to negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction effect disappears after controlling for either series alone.
